taxonomy: Eriocaulon australasicum is classified in the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. This family is further classified in the order Poales, which comprises monocotyledonous plants that produce flowers with three petals. The order Poales is part of the clade commelinids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Poaceae and Bromeliaceae. Eriocaulaceae is a relatively small family of approximately 750 species, with Eriocaulon australasicum being one of them. The classification of Eriocaulon australasicum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
